source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The square-box in Fig. 3.71 represents an unknown linear lumpedconstant passive network. The source of emf at the left is assumed to have zero internal impedance. It is known that if the input emf $e_{i}(t)$ is a step function, i.e., $e_{i}(t)=\begin{cases}0 & t\leq 0\\ A & t>0\end{cases}$ then the open-circuit (no-load) output voltage $e_{0}(t)$ is of the form $e_{0}(t)=\begin{cases}0, & t\leq 0\\ \frac{1}{2}A[1-\exp(-t/\tau)], & t>0\end{cases}$ where the constant $\tau$ has the value $\tau=1.2\times10^{-4} \;\mathrm{s}$. Find the open-circuit (no load) output voltage $e_{0}(t)$ when the input is given by $e_{i}(t)=4\cos(\omega t)$ volts where $\omega$ corresponds to the frequency 1500 cycles/sec.
extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object string representations, not explicit file paths or URLs
question explicitly references Fig. 3.71 and the record marks vision_relevance as essential

issues: metadata.image_paths contains PIL object string representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs

image_dependency: 题干显式引用 'Fig. 3.71'，且字段 `requires_image=true`、`text_dominant=false` 表明上游判断存在图像依赖。
但题干已文字给出关键动态关系：阶跃输入与开路输出的解析式以及时间常数，求解核心主要可由文本完成。
图像看起来更像网络结构示意而非复杂图表读数，因此保守评为中等偏高而非极高。
multi_step: 需要先由阶跃响应识别/推导该线性无源网络的传递特性，再对正弦输入求对应输出，属于至少两步推理。
题干同时结合系统性质（linear lumped-constant passive network）、时域响应、频率信息（1500 cycles/sec）与目标输出表达式，推理链较长。
非选择题，答案不是直接从图中读取，通常需要计算或等价变换。
verifiability: 答案类型为 `short_text`，目标应为明确的解析表达式，便于与标准形式或等价变形进行核验。
题干给出充分参数：阶跃响应形式、`tau=1.2e-4 s`、输入 `4 cos(omega t)`、频率 1500 Hz，后续可基于系统理论检查答案是否一致。
`asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true`，题目与答案资产登记完整，支持后续规则化验证。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: The square-box in Fig. 3.71 represents an unknown linear lumpedconstant passive network. The source of emf at the left is assumed to have zero internal impedance. It is known that if the input emf $e_{i}(t)$ is a step function, i.e., $e_{i}(t)=\begin{cases}0 & t\leq 0\\ A & t>0\end{cases}$ then the open-circuit (no-load) output voltage $e_{0}(t)$ is of the form $e_{0}(t)=\begin{cases}0, & t\leq 0\\ \frac{1}{2}A[1-\exp(-t/\tau)], & t>0\end{cases}$ where the constant $\tau$ has the value $\tau=1.2\times10^{-4} \;\mathrm{s}$. Find the open-circuit (no load) output voltage $e_{0}(t)$ when the input is given by $e_{i}(t)=4\cos(\omega t)$ volts where $\omega$ corresponds to the frequency 1500 cycles/sec.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object string representations, not explicit file paths or URLs
question explicitly references Fig. 3.71 and the record marks vision_relevance as essential
metadata.reasoning_chain: We first use the Laplace transform to find the transmission function $H(s)$ of the network in the frequency domain. The Laplace transform of the equation $e_{i}(t)=A \cdot U(t)$ is $E_{i}(s)=A/S$. Similarly, the Laplace transform of the output $e_{0}(t)$ is $E_{0}(s)=\frac{1}{2}A\left[\frac{1}{S}-\frac{1}{S+1/\tau}\right]$. Hence the transmission function is $H(s)=\frac{E_{0}(s)}{E_{i}(s)}=\frac{\frac{1}{2\tau}}{s+\frac{1}{\tau}}$. The Laplace transform of the new input $e_{i}(t)=4\cos(\omega t)$ is $E_{i}(s)=\frac{4s}{\omega^{2}+s^{2}}$ giving the output as $E_{0}(s)=E_{i}(s)\cdot H(s)=\frac{4s}{\omega^{2}+s^{2}}\times\frac{\frac{1}{2\tau}}{s+\frac{1}{\tau}}=\frac{2}{\tau}\left[\frac{1}{s+\frac{1}{\tau}}\cdot\frac{-\frac{1}{\tau}}{\omega^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{\tau}\right)^{2}}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{s-i\omega}\times\frac{1}{i\omega+\frac{1}{\tau}}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{s+i\omega}\times\frac{1}{i\omega+\frac{1}{\tau}}\right]$, where $\omega\tau=2\pi\times1500\times1.2\times10^{-4}\approx1$. The reverse transformation of $E_{0}(s)$ gives the open-circuit output voltage $V_{0}(t)=\frac{2}{\tau}\left[\frac{-\frac{1}{\tau}}{\omega^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{\tau}\right)^{2}}e^{-t/\tau}+\frac{\frac{1}{\tau}\cos(\omega t)+\omega\sin(\omega t)}{\omega^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{\tau}\right)^{2}}\right]\simeq-e^{-t/\tau}+\cos(\omega t)+\sin(\omega t)$

problem_main_record.raw_question_text: The square-box in Fig. 3.71 represents an unknown linear lumpedconstant passive network. The source of emf at the left is assumed to have zero internal impedance. It is known that if the input emf $e_{i}(t)$ is a step function, i.e., $e_{i}(t)=\begin{cases}0 & t\leq 0\\ A & t>0\end{cases}$ then the open-circuit (no-load) output voltage $e_{0}(t)$ is of the form $e_{0}(t)=\begin{cases}0, & t\leq 0\\ \frac{1}{2}A[1-\exp(-t/\tau)], & t>0\end{cases}$ where the constant $\tau$ has the value $\tau=1.2\times10^{-4} \;\mathrm{s}$. Find the open-circuit (no load) output voltage $e_{0}(t)$ when the input is given by $e_{i}(t)=4\cos(\omega t)$ volts where $\omega$ corresponds to the frequency 1500 cycles/sec.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: We first use the Laplace transform to find the transmission function $H(s)$ of the network in the frequency domain. The Laplace transform of the equation $e_{i}(t)=A \cdot U(t)$ is $E_{i}(s)=A/S$. Similarly, the Laplace transform of the output $e_{0}(t)$ is $E_{0}(s)=\frac{1}{2}A\left[\frac{1}{S}-\frac{1}{S+1/\tau}\right]$. Hence the transmission function is $H(s)=\frac{E_{0}(s)}{E_{i}(s)}=\frac{\frac{1}{2\tau}}{s+\frac{1}{\tau}}$. The Laplace transform of the new input $e_{i}(t)=4\cos(\omega t)$ is $E_{i}(s)=\frac{4s}{\omega^{2}+s^{2}}$ giving the output as $E_{0}(s)=E_{i}(s)\cdot H(s)=\frac{4s}{\omega^{2}+s^{2}}\times\frac{\frac{1}{2\tau}}{s+\frac{1}{\tau}}=\frac{2}{\tau}\left[\frac{1}{s+\frac{1}{\tau}}\cdot\frac{-\frac{1}{\tau}}{\omega^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{\tau}\right)^{2}}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{s-i\omega}\times\frac{1}{i\omega+\frac{1}{\tau}}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{s+i\omega}\times\frac{1}{i\omega+\frac{1}{\tau}}\right]$, where $\omega\tau=2\pi\times1500\times1.2\times10^{-4}\approx1$. The reverse transformation of $E_{0}(s)$ gives the open-circuit output voltage $V_{0}(t)=\frac{2}{\tau}\left[\frac{-\frac{1}{\tau}}{\omega^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{\tau}\right)^{2}}e^{-t/\tau}+\frac{\frac{1}{\tau}\cos(\omega t)+\omega\sin(\omega t)}{\omega^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{\tau}\right)^{2}}\right]\simeq-e^{-t/\tau}+\cos(\omega t)+\sin(\omega t)$

It is known that if the input emf $e_i(t)$ is a step function, i.e., $e_i(t)=\begin{cases}0 & t\leq 0\\ A & t>0\end{cases}$, then the open-circuit (no-load) output voltage $e_0(t)$ is of the form $e_0(t)=\begin{cases}0, & t\leq 0\\ \frac{1}{2}A[1-\exp(-t/\tau)], & t>0\end{cases}$, where the constant $\tau$ has the value $\tau=1.2\times10^{-4}\,\mathrm{s}$. Find the open-circuit (no-load) output voltage $e_0(t)$ when the input is given by $e_i(t)=4\cos(\omega t)$ volts, where $\omega$ corresponds to the frequency 1500 cycles/sec.

Find the open-circuit (no load) output voltage $e_{0}(t)$ when the input is given by $e_{i}(t)=4\cos(\omega t)$ volts where $\omega$ corresponds to the frequency 1500 cycles/sec.
